89: pr_details: Mapping of pr_number -> PRDetails for get_pr() and get_pr_for_branch() 90- prs_by_branch: Mapping of branch name -> PRDetails (simpler than prs + pr_details) 91- workflow_runs: List of WorkflowRun objects to return from list_workflow_runs 92- workflow_runs_by_node_id: Mapping of GraphQL node_id -> WorkflowRun for 93- get_workflow_runs_by_node_ids() 94- run_logs: Mapping of run_id -> log string 95- pr_plan_linkages: Mapping of plan_number -> list[PullRequestInfo] 96- polled_run_id: Run ID to return from poll_for_workflow_run (None for timeout) 97- authenticated: Whether gh CLI is authenticated (default True for test convenience) 98- auth_username: Username returned by check_auth_status() (default "test-user") 99- auth_hostname: Hostname returned by check_auth_status() (default "github.com") 100- issues_gateway: Optional GitHubIssues implementation. If None, creates 101- FakeGitHubIssues with default empty state. 102- issues_data: List of IssueInfo objects for get_issues_with_pr_linkages() 103- pr_titles: Mapping of pr_number -> title for explicit title storage 104- pr_bodies_by_number: Mapping of pr_number -> body for explicit body storage -- 298: get_pr_for_branch() work after creation without manual setup. 299- 300- Returns: 301- An incrementing fake PR number for testing 302- """ 303- pr_number = self._next_pr_number 304- self._next_pr_number += 1 305- self._created_prs.append((branch, title, body, base, draft)) 306- 307- effective_base = base if base is not None else "main" 308- pr_url = ( 309- f"https://github.com/{self._repo_info.owner}/{self._repo_info.name}/pull/{pr_number}" 310- )

37→class FakeGitHub(GitHub): 38→ """In-memory fake implementation of GitHub operations. 39→ 40→ This class has NO public setup methods. All state is provided via constructor 41→ using keyword arguments with sensible defaults (empty dicts). 42→ """ 43→ 44→ def __init__( 45→ self, 46→ *, 47→ repo_info: RepoInfo | None = None, 48→ prs: dict[str, PullRequestInfo] | None = None, 49→ pr_bases: dict[int, str] | None = None, 50→ pr_details: dict[int, PRDetails] | None = None, 51→ prs_by_branch: dict[str, PRDetails] | None = None, 52→ workflow_runs: list[WorkflowRun] | None = None, 53→ workflow_runs_by_node_id: dict[str, WorkflowRun] | None = None, 54→ run_logs: dict[str, str] | None = None, 55→ pr_plan_linkages: dict[int, list[PullRequestInfo]] | None = None, 56→ polled_run_id: str | None = None, 57→ authenticated: bool = True, 58→ auth_username: str | None = "test-user", 59→ auth_hostname: str | None = "github.com", 60→ issues_gateway: GitHubIssues | None = None, 61→ issues_data: list[IssueInfo] | None = None, 62→ pr_titles: dict[int, str] | None = None, 63→ pr_bodies_by_number: dict[int, str] | None = None, 64→ pr_diffs: dict[int, str] | None = None, 65→ pr_changed_files: dict[int, list[str]] | None = None, 66→ merge_should_succeed: bool = True,

session.user:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The conversation is summarized below: Analysis: Let me chronologically analyze the conversation:
1. The user invoked `/erk:plan-implement 8841` which is a command to implement a plan from GitHub issue #8841.
2. The plan was set up via `erk exec setup-impl --issue 8841`, creating an implementation environment on branch `plnd/add-erk-reconcile-rename-d-03-06-0728`.
3. The plan (#8841) is titled "Plan: `erk reconcile` + Rename `reconcile-with-remote` → `diverge-fix`" and has 4 parts: - Part 1: Rename `reconcile-with-remote` → `diverge-fix` (mechanical rename across many files) - Part 2: Git layer extensions (gone field on BranchSyncInfo, fetch_prune method) - Part 3: New `erk reconcile` command (pipeline + CLI + registration) - Part 4: Tests
4. Implementation order specified in plan: Part 2 → Part 1 → Part 3A-B → Part 3C-D → Part 4
5. I loaded skills (dignified-python, Python 3.11 version), launched 3 explore agents to understand the codebase.
6. PART 2 COMPLETED: Git layer extensions - Added `gone: bool = False` to BranchSyncInfo dataclass - Added `gone = "gone" in track` parsing in real branch ops - Added `fetch_prune` abstract method to GitRemoteOps ABC - Added `fetch_prune` to all 5 implementations (real, fake, dry_run, printing)
7. PART 1 COMPLETED: Rename reconcile-with-remote → diverge-fix - 4 file renames via git mv - Content updates in renamed files (command name, function names, imports) - Content-only updates in 12+ files (imports, references, docs) - Final grep confirmed only CHANGELOG.md retains old name (as intended)
8. PART 3A-B COMPLETED: Reconcile pipeline logic + learn extraction - Created `src/erk/cli/commands/reconcile_pipeline.py` with: - ReconcileBranchInfo and ReconcileResult dataclasses - detect_merged_branches() function - process_merged_branch() function - _cleanup_branch() helper - Added `_create_learn_pr_for_merged_branch()` to `src/erk/cli/commands/land_learn.py`
9. PART 3C-D COMPLETED: CLI command + registration - Created `src/erk/cli/commands/reconcile_cmd.py` with reconcile click command - Registered in `src/erk/cli/cli.py`
10. PART 4 IN PROGRESS: Tests for reconcile - Was researching test patterns, exploring FakeGit, FakeGitHub, test context builders - Had not yet written the test file when summary was requested
Errors encountered: - Some Edit calls failed with "File has not been read yet" for files that were only partially read via offset/limit. Fixed by re-reading those files and retrying the edits.
No user feedback messages were received - the user only triggered the plan-implement command and let me work. The system-reminder messages about TodoWrite were system-generated.
Summary: 1. Primary Request and Intent: The user invoked `/erk:plan-implement 8841` to implement GitHub plan #8841 titled "Plan: `erk reconcile` + Rename `reconcile-with-remote` → `diverge-fix`". This is a multi-part implementation that: - Renames the existing `erk pr reconcile-with-remote` command to `erk pr diverge-fix` (freeing up the "reconcile" name) - Extends the git layer with a `gone` field on `BranchSyncInfo` and a `fetch_prune` method on `GitRemoteOps` - Creates a new first-class `erk reconcile` command that detects locally-tracked branches merged remotely (outside `erk land`) and runs post-merge lifecycle operations (learn PR, objective update, cleanup) - Writes comprehensive tests
2. Key Technical Concepts: - **Gateway pattern with strategy/composition**: Git operations use ABC → Real → Fake → DryRun → Printing implementations - **Discriminated union returns** (LBYL over EAFP): e.g., `PRDetails | PRNotFound` - **Frozen dataclasses** for immutable state threading - **Fail-open patterns**: Learn PR creation and objective updates never block the main operation - **Click CLI framework** with command groups and registration - **FakeGit/FakeGitHub** testing infrastructure with constructor injection and mutation tracking - **Python 3.11** with `X | None` union syntax, `Self` type - **Branch sync info**: `git for-each-ref` output parsing including `[gone]` tracking status - **Worktree pool management**: Slot assignment/unassignment for multi-worktree workflows - **Plan backend**: Resolves plan IDs for branches, creates learn PRs
3. Files and Code Sections:
- **`packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/git/abc.py`** (MODIFIED) - Added `gone: bool = False` field to `BranchSyncInfo` frozen dataclass ```python @dataclass(frozen=True) class BranchSyncInfo: branch: str upstream: str | None ahead: int behind: int gone: bool = False # True if upstream tracking branch has been deleted ```
- **`packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/git/branch_ops/real.py`** (MODIFIED) - Added `gone = "gone" in track` parsing in `get_all_branch_sync_info()` (around line 316) ```python gone = False if track: gone = "gone" in track # Parse "[ahead N, behind M]" or "[ahead N]" or "[behind M]" ... sync_info[branch] = BranchSyncInfo(branch=branch, upstream=upstream, ahead=ahead, behind=behind, gone=gone) ```
- **`packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/git/remote_ops/abc.py`** (MODIFIED) - Added `fetch_prune` abstract method before `fetch_branch` ```python @abstractmethod def fetch_prune(self, repo_root: Path, remote: str) -> None: """Fetch from a remote and prune deleted tracking branches.""" ... ```
- **`packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/git/remote_ops/real.py`** (MODIFIED) - Added `fetch_prune` implementation using `run_subprocess_with_context` ```python def fetch_prune(self, repo_root: Path, remote: str) -> None: run_subprocess_with_context( cmd=["git", "fetch", "--prune", remote], operation_context=f"fetch --prune from remote '{remote}'", cwd=repo_root, timeout=_GIT_NETWORK_TIMEOUT, env=copied_env_for_git_subprocess(), ) ```
- **`packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/git/remote_ops/fake.py`** (MODIFIED) - Added `fetch_prune_raises` constructor parameter, `_fetch_prune_calls` tracking list, `fetch_prune()` method, and `fetch_prune_calls` property ```python def fetch_prune(self, repo_root: Path, remote: str) -> None: if self._fetch_prune_raises is not None: raise self._fetch_prune_raises self._fetch_prune_calls.append((repo_root, remote)) ```
- **`packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/git/remote_ops/dry_run.py`** (MODIFIED) - Added no-op `fetch_prune` method
- **`packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/git/remote_ops/printing.py`** (MODIFIED) - Added `fetch_prune` with formatted output and delegation
- **`.claude/commands/erk/reconcile-with-remote.md` → `.claude/commands/erk/diverge-fix.md`** (RENAMED + MODIFIED) - Updated description, title, and stash message references
- **`src/erk/cli/commands/pr/reconcile_with_remote_cmd.py` → `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/diverge_fix_cmd.py`** (RENAMED + MODIFIED) - Renamed function `pr_reconcile_with_remote` → `pr_diverge_fix` - Updated click command name `"reconcile-with-remote"` → `"diverge-fix"` - Updated import `stream_reconcile_with_remote` → `stream_diverge_fix` - Updated all docstrings and error messages
- **`tests/commands/pr/test_reconcile_with_remote.py` → `tests/commands/pr/test_diverge_fix.py`** (RENAMED + MODIFIED) - All test names, command invocations, and assertions updated from `reconcile-with-remote` to `diverge-fix`
- **`docs/learned/cli/commands/pr-reconcile-with-remote.md` → `docs/learned/cli/commands/pr-diverge-fix.md`** (RENAMED + REWRITTEN)
- **`src/erk/cli/commands/pr/__init__.py`** (MODIFIED) - Import changed: `from erk.cli.commands.pr.diverge_fix_cmd import pr_diverge_fix` - Registration: `pr_group.add_command(pr_diverge_fix, name="diverge-fix")`
- **`src/erk/cli/output.py`** (MODIFIED) - Renamed `ReconcileWithRemoteResult` → `DivergenceFixResult` - Renamed `stream_reconcile_with_remote` → `stream_diverge_fix` - Updated all internal command references from `/erk:reconcile-with-remote` to `/erk:diverge-fix`
- **`src/erk/cli/ensure.py`** (MODIFIED) - Comment update line 126
- **`src/erk/cli/commands/pr/submit_pipeline.py`** (MODIFIED) - Error message updated to `erk pr diverge-fix`
- **`tests/commands/pr/test_submit.py`** (MODIFIED) - Assertion updated line 1790
- **12 documentation files** (MODIFIED) - All references to `reconcile-with-remote` updated to `diverge-fix`: - `docs/learned/cli/commands/index.md` - `docs/learned/cli/index.md` - `docs/learned/cli/command-organization.md` - `docs/learned/erk/graphite-divergence-detection.md` - `docs/learned/architecture/tripwires.md` - `docs/learned/architecture/git-graphite-quirks.md` - `docs/learned/pr-operations/draft-pr-handling.md` - `.claude/commands/erk/pr-address.md` - `docs/howto/conflict-resolution.md` - `docs/howto/pr-checkout-sync.md`
- **`src/erk/cli/commands/reconcile_pipeline.py`** (CREATED) - Core pipeline module with detection and processing logic ```python @dataclass(frozen=True) class ReconcileBranchInfo: branch: str pr_number: int pr_title: str | None worktree_path: Path | None plan_id: str | None objective_number: int | None
@dataclass(frozen=True) class ReconcileResult: branch: str learn_created: bool objective_updated: bool cleaned_up: bool error: str | None
def detect_merged_branches(ctx, *, repo_root, main_repo_root) -> list[ReconcileBranchInfo]: # 1. fetch_prune, 2. filter gone=True, 3. exclude trunk, # 4. check PR state MERGED, 5. resolve plan/objective/worktree
def process_merged_branch(ctx, info, *, main_repo_root, repo, cwd, dry_run, skip_learn) -> ReconcileResult: # 1. Learn PR (fail-open), 2. Objective update (fail-open), 3. Cleanup
def _cleanup_branch(ctx, *, info, main_repo_root, repo) -> None: # Unassign slot, ensure not checked out, delete branch, remove worktree ```
- **`src/erk/cli/commands/land_learn.py`** (MODIFIED) - Added `_create_learn_pr_for_merged_branch()` function that wraps learn PR creation without requiring `LandState` ```python def _create_learn_pr_for_merged_branch( ctx: ErkContext, *, plan_id: str, merged_pr_number: int, main_repo_root: Path, cwd: Path, ) -> None: ``` - This is a thin wrapper that replicates the logic of `_create_learn_pr_impl` but constructs needed state locally
- **`src/erk/cli/commands/reconcile_cmd.py`** (CREATED) - Click command with `--force`, `--dry-run`, `--skip-learn` options - Flow: discover repo → detect merged → display table → confirm → process each → pull trunk → display results ```python @click.command("reconcile") @click.option("--force", is_flag=True) @click.option("--dry-run", is_flag=True) @click.option("--skip-learn", is_flag=True) @click.pass_obj def reconcile(ctx: ErkContext, *, force, dry_run, skip_learn) -> None: ```
- **`src/erk/cli/cli.py`** (MODIFIED) - Added import: `from erk.cli.commands.reconcile_cmd import reconcile` - Added registration: `cli.add_command(reconcile)` after `cli.add_command(land)`
4. Errors and Fixes: - **"File has not been read yet" errors on Edit**: When trying to edit `docs/learned/pr-operations/draft-pr-handling.md`, `docs/learned/architecture/git-graphite-quirks.md`, and `docs/learned/architecture/tripwires.md`, the Edit tool rejected the calls because those files had only been partially read (via offset/limit from an explore agent). Fixed by explicitly reading those files with the Read tool before retrying the Edit. - No user feedback was received about errors - the implementation proceeded autonomously.
5. Problem Solving: - Efficiently parallelized exploration with 3 explore agents to understand git layer, reconcile-with-remote files, and land command infrastructure simultaneously - Used `replace_all` mode for bulk string replacements in the test rename file - Final grep verification confirmed all `reconcile-with-remote` references were cleaned up (only CHANGELOG.md remains, intentionally preserved per plan) - The learn extraction was designed to avoid requiring `LandState` by creating a standalone wrapper function
6. All User Messages: - The user's only explicit message was invoking the `/erk:plan-implement 8841` command. All subsequent messages were system-generated reminders (TodoWrite reminders) or tool results. No direct user feedback was provided during implementation.
7. Pending Tasks: - **Part 4: Tests for reconcile** - Write `tests/commands/test_reconcile.py` with tests per the plan (13 test cases specified) - **Run CI iteratively** - Check for post-plan-implement-ci hook or use CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md CI commands - **Submit PR** - `erk pr submit` and lifecycle signals - **Steps 9-14** of the plan-implement workflow (signal ended, upload session, cleanup impl context, CI, submit PR, output)
8. Current Work: I was in the middle of **Part 4: Writing tests for the reconcile command**. I had just finished researching the test infrastructure by reading: - `tests/commands/pr/test_diverge_fix.py` (pattern for test structure) - `build_workspace_test_context` function signature - `FakeGit` constructor with `branch_sync_info` parameter (how to inject `gone=True`) - `FakeGitHub` constructor with `prs_by_branch` parameter (how to set up PR state as MERGED) - `FakeGitBranchOps.get_all_branch_sync_info` (returns `self._branch_sync_info.get(repo_root, {})`) - `FakeGitHub.get_pr_for_branch` (checks `_prs_by_branch` first)
I was about to create `tests/commands/test_reconcile.py` with the 13 test cases specified in the plan.
9. Optional Next Step: Create `tests/commands/test_reconcile.py` with integration tests for the reconcile command. The plan specifies these test cases: - `test_detects_merged_branches` - FakeGit `gone=True` + FakeGitHub `MERGED` → detected - `test_skips_closed_not_merged` - `gone=True` but PR state `CLOSED` → skipped - `test_skips_normal_branches` - Branches without `gone` → skipped - `test_skips_trunk` - Trunk with `gone=True` → skipped - `test_dry_run_no_mutations` - `--dry-run` shows plan, no deletions - `test_force_skips_confirmation` - `--force` processes without prompting - `test_cleans_up_branch` - Branch deleted after processing - `test_nothing_to_reconcile` - No `gone` branches → "Nothing to reconcile" - Plus tests for learn failure, objective failure, slot unassignment, trunk pull
Key patterns needed: Use `FakeGit(branch_sync_info={repo_root: {"branch": BranchSyncInfo(..., gone=True)}})` and `FakeGitHub(prs_by_branch={"branch": PRDetails(..., state="MERGED")})` with `build_workspace_test_context` and `CliRunner`.. Please continue the conversation from where we left it off without asking the user any further questions. Continue with the last task that you were asked to work on.
session.assistant: I need to continue with Part 4: Writing tests for the reconcile command. Let me first read the key files I need to understand the test infrastructure, then write the tests.

session.tool_result:
Perfect! Now I have enough information. Let me compile a comprehensive report:
---
# Test Infrastructure Guide for `erk reconcile` CLI Command
## 1. Test File Location & Imports Pattern
Based on `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/test_wt_checkout.py`:
```python """Tests for erk reconcile command."""
from pathlib import Path from click.testing import CliRunner
from erk.cli.cli import cli from erk_shared.gateway.git.abc import WorktreeInfo, BranchSyncInfo from erk_shared.gateway.git.fake import FakeGit from erk_shared.gateway.github.fake import FakeGitHub from erk_shared.gateway.github.types import PRDetails from tests.test_utils.env_helpers import erk_inmem_env ```
## 2. Test Context Builder Function
**Location**: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/test_utils/context_builders.py`
**Signature**: ```python
... [412 lines omitted] ...
add_worktree_error: str | None = None, remove_worktree_error: str | None = None, except RuntimeError: catch_exceptions=False, catch_exceptions=False

session.tool_result:
1→"""High-level git operations interface. 2→ 3→This module provides a clean abstraction over git subprocess calls, making the 4→codebase more testable and maintainable. 5→ 6→Architecture: 7→- Git: Abstract base class defining the interface 8→- RealGit: Production implementation using subprocess 9→- Standalone functions: Convenience wrappers delegating to module singleton 10→""" 11→ 12→from __future__ import annotations 13→ 14→from abc import ABC, abstractmethod 15→from dataclasses import dataclass 16→from pathlib import Path 17→from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, NamedTuple 18→ 19→if TYPE_CHECKING: 20→ from erk_shared.gateway.git.analysis_ops.abc import GitAnalysisOps 21→ from erk_shared.gateway.git.branch_ops.abc import GitBranchOps 22→ from erk_shared.gateway.git.commit_ops.abc import GitCommitOps 23→ from erk_shared.gateway.git.config_ops.abc import GitConfigOps 24→ from erk_shared.gateway.git.rebase_ops.abc import GitRebaseOps 25→ from erk_shared.gateway.git.remote_ops.abc import GitRemoteOps 26→ from erk_shared.gateway.git.repo_ops.abc import GitRepoOps 27→ from erk_shared.gateway.git.status_ops.abc import GitStatusOps 28→ from erk_shared.gateway.git.tag_ops.abc import GitTagOps 29→ from erk_shared.gateway.git.worktree.abc import Worktree 30→

1→"""Reconcile locally-tracked branches that were merged remotely. 2→ 3→Detects branches whose PRs were merged outside `erk land` (e.g., via GitHub 4→web UI, mobile, or API) and runs the same post-merge lifecycle: learn PR 5→creation, objective updates, and branch/worktree cleanup. 6→""" 7→ 8→import click 9→from rich.console import Console 10→from rich.table import Table 11→ 12→from erk.cli.commands.reconcile_pipeline import ( 13→ ReconcileBranchInfo, 14→ ReconcileResult, 15→ detect_merged_branches, 16→ process_merged_branch, 17→) 18→from erk.cli.core import discover_repo_context 19→from erk.core.context import ErkContext 20→from erk_shared.output.output import user_output 21→ 22→ 23→@click.command("reconcile") 24→@click.option("--force", is_flag=True, help="Skip confirmation prompts") 25→@click.option("--dry-run", is_flag=True, help="Preview without making changes") 26→@click.option("--skip-learn", is_flag=True, help="Skip creating learn plans") 27→@click.pass_obj 28→def reconcile(ctx: ErkContext, *, force: bool, dry_run: bool, skip_learn: bool) -> None: 29→ """Reconcile branches merged outside erk land. 30→
... [143 lines omitted] ...
101→ except Exception: 102→ user_output(click.style("Warning: ", fg="yellow") + "Failed to pull trunk") 142→ successes = sum(1 for r in results if r.error is None) 143→ failures = len(results) - successes 146→ status = click.style("ok", fg="green") if result.error is None else click.style("FAIL", fg="red") 157→ if result.error is not None: 158→ parts.append(f"- {result.error}") 162→ if failures == 0: 166→ click.style(f"{successes} succeeded, {failures} failed.", fg="yellow", bold=True)

1→"""Detection and processing pipeline for reconciling merged branches. 2→ 3→Detects branches whose remote tracking refs have been pruned (gone=True) 4→and whose PRs are in MERGED state. For each, runs post-merge lifecycle 5→operations: learn PR creation, objective update, and branch/worktree cleanup. 6→""" 7→ 8→from __future__ import annotations 9→ 10→import logging 11→from dataclasses import dataclass 12→from pathlib import Path 13→ 14→import click 15→ 16→from erk.cli.commands.land_cmd import _ensure_branch_not_checked_out 17→from erk.cli.commands.land_learn import _create_learn_pr_for_merged_branch 18→from erk.cli.commands.objective_helpers import ( 19→ get_objective_for_branch, 20→ run_objective_update_after_land, 21→) 22→from erk.cli.commands.slot.common import find_branch_assignment 23→from erk.cli.commands.slot.unassign_cmd import execute_unassign 24→from erk.cli.commands.wt.delete_cmd import _prune_worktrees_safe 25→from erk.core.context import ErkContext 26→from erk.core.worktree_pool import load_pool_state 27→from erk_shared.context.types import RepoContext 28→from erk_shared.gateway.github.types import PRNotFound 29→from erk_shared.output.output import user_output 30→
... [224 lines omitted] ...
54→ error: str | None # None = success 137→ Each step is fail-open: errors on one step don't prevent the next. 154→ error: str | None = None 162→ error=None, 176→ except Exception as exc: 178→ click.style("Warning: ", fg="yellow") 179→ + f"Learn PR failed for {info.branch}: {exc}" 194→ except Exception as exc: 196→ click.style("Warning: ", fg="yellow") 197→ + f"Objective update failed for {info.branch}: {exc}" 204→ except Exception as exc: 205→ error = f"Cleanup failed for {info.branch}: {exc}" 206→ user_output(click.style("Warning: ", fg="yellow") + error) 213→ error=error,

1→"""Centralized test environment helpers for simulating erk scenarios. 2→ 3→This module provides helpers for setting up realistic erk test environments 4→with Click's CliRunner. It provides two patterns: 5→ 6→1. erk_isolated_fs_env(): Filesystem-based (uses isolated_filesystem(), 7→ creates real directories) 8→2. erk_inmem_env(): In-memory (uses fakes only, no filesystem I/O) 9→ 10→Key Components: 11→ - ErkIsolatedFsEnv: Helper class for filesystem-based testing 12→ - ErkInMemEnv: Helper class for in-memory testing 13→ - erk_isolated_fs_env(): Context manager for filesystem-based tests 14→ - erk_inmem_env(): Context manager for in-memory tests 15→ 16→Usage Pattern: 17→ 18→ Before (raw isolated_filesystem pattern - 20-30 lines per test): 19→ ```python 20→ def test_something() -> None: 21→ runner = CliRunner() 22→ with runner.isolated_filesystem(): 23→ cwd = Path.cwd() 24→ git_dir = cwd / ".git" 25→ git_dir.mkdir() 26→ erk_root = cwd / "erks" 27→ erk_root.mkdir() 28→ 29→ git = FakeGit(git_common_dirs={cwd: git_dir}) 30→ global_config = GlobalConfig.test(...)
... [1195 lines omitted] ...

1→"""Type definitions for GitHub operations.""" 2→ 3→from dataclasses import dataclass, field 4→from datetime import UTC, datetime 5→from pathlib import Path 6→from typing import Any, Literal, TypedDict 7→ 8→from erk_shared.non_ideal_state import EnsurableResult 9→ 10→PRState = Literal["OPEN", "MERGED", "CLOSED"] 11→ 12→MergeableStatus = Literal["MERGEABLE", "CONFLICTING", "UNKNOWN"] 13→ 14→ 15→class StatusCheckRollupData(TypedDict, total=False): 16→ state: str 17→ contexts: dict[str, Any] 18→ 19→ 20→def _epoch_sentinel() -> datetime: 21→ """Return the epoch sentinel datetime used as default for missing PR timestamps.""" 22→ return datetime(2000, 1, 1, tzinfo=UTC) 23→ 24→ 25→@dataclass(frozen=True) 26→class BodyText: 27→ """Body content as inline text. 28→ 29→ Used when the body content is a string that should be passed directly 30→ to the GitHub API using -f body={content} syntax.

1→from pathlib import Path 2→ 3→from erk.cli.ensure import Ensure 4→from erk.core.context import ErkContext 5→from erk.core.repo_discovery import RepoContext, discover_repo_or_sentinel 6→ 7→ 8→def discover_repo_context(ctx: ErkContext, start: Path) -> RepoContext: 9→ """Walk up from `start` to find a directory containing `.git`. 10→ 11→ Returns a RepoContext pointing to the repo root and the global worktrees directory 12→ for this repository. 13→ Raises FileNotFoundError if not inside a git repo. 14→ 15→ Note: Properly handles git worktrees by finding the main repository root, 16→ not the worktree's .git file. 17→ """ 18→ if ctx.global_config is None: 19→ raise FileNotFoundError("Global config not found. Run 'erk init' to create it.") 20→ 21→ result = discover_repo_or_sentinel(start, ctx.global_config.erk_root, ctx.git) 22→ if isinstance(result, RepoContext): 23→ return result 24→ raise FileNotFoundError(result.message) 25→ 26→ 27→def worktree_path_for(worktrees_dir: Path, name: str) -> Path: 28→ """Return the absolute path for a named worktree within worktrees directory. 29→ 30→ Note: Does not handle 'root' as a special case. Commands that support

134:class PRDetails(EnsurableResult): 135- """Comprehensive PR information from a single GitHub API call. 136- 137- This dataclass contains all commonly-needed PR fields, allowing call sites 138- to fetch everything they need in one API call instead of multiple separate 139- calls for title, body, base branch, etc. 140- """ 141- 142- # Identity 143- number: int 144- url: str 145- 146- # Content 147- title: str 148- body: str 149- 150- # State 151- state: str # "OPEN", "MERGED", "CLOSED" 152- is_draft: bool 153- 154- # Structure 155- base_ref_name: str 156- head_ref_name: str 157- is_cross_repository: bool 158- 159- # Mergeability 160- mergeable: MergeableStatus 161- merge_state_status: str # "CLEAN", "BLOCKED", "UNSTABLE", "DIRTY" 162- 163- # Metadata

session.assistant: The test env's `RepoContext` doesn't set `main_repo_root`. But the `reconcile_cmd` uses `repo.main_repo_root`. Let me check if `RepoContext.__post_init__` defaults it:
session.tool_result:
103→class RepoContext: 104→ """Represents a git repo root and its managed worktrees directory. 105→ 106→ Attributes: 107→ root: The actual working tree root (where git commands run). 108→ For worktrees, this is the worktree directory. 109→ For main repos, this equals main_repo_root. 110→ repo_name: Name of the repository (derived from main_repo_root). 111→ repo_dir: Path to erk metadata directory (~/.erk/repos/<repo-name>). 112→ worktrees_dir: Path to worktrees directory (~/.erk/repos/<repo-name>/worktrees). 113→ main_repo_root: The main repository root (for consistent metadata paths). 114→ For worktrees, this is the parent repo's root directory. 115→ For main repos, this equals root. 116→ Defaults to root for backwards compatibility. 117→ github: GitHub repository identity, if available. 118→ """ 119→ 120→ root: Path 121→ repo_name: str 122→ repo_dir: Path # ~/.erk/repos/<repo-name> 123→ worktrees_dir: Path # ~/.erk/repos/<repo-name>/worktrees 124→ pool_json_path: Path # ~/.erk/repos/<repo-name>/pool.json 125→ main_repo_root: Path | None = None # Defaults to root for backwards compatibility 126→ github: GitHubRepoId | None = None # None if not a GitHub repo or no remote 127→ 128→ def __post_init__(self) -> None: 129→ """Set main_repo_root to root if not provided.""" 130→ if self.main_repo_root is None: 131→ # Use object.__setattr__ because dataclass is frozen 132→ object.__setattr__(self, "main_repo_root", self.root)
... [15 lines omitted] ...
